revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:御願塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ogan-zuka Kofun | location = {{ill|Itami|qid=Q335444|1=Itami}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.766027777778|N|135.41458888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped KofunItami City Board of Education, "Excavation Report of Ogan-zuka Kofun, Itami City, Hyōgo Prefecture," 2008, p.2 | built = Late 5th century | Interred Individual = Local ruler? | area = Length: 52 m, Height: 7 m | designation1 = Prefectural Historic Site, 1966 (Showa 41) | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, Sue pottery | image = File:御願塚主墳正面.jpg }} '''Gogazuka Kofun''' (御願塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Itami|en|Itami|qid=Q335444|lt=Itami|1=Itami}}, Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan. It is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. On March 22, 1966 (Showa 41), it was Historic Site1 as a Prefectural Historic Site and designation1.[http://www.city.itami.lg.jp/home/ED/EDSHOGAI/EDSYAKAI/0003086/0002488.html#kinenbutsu Hyōgo Prefecture Designated Historic Site] (Itami City). {{osm box|w|469890031}} == Overview == The Ogan-zuka Kofun is believed to be the northernmost point of the now mostly lost {{ill|Tsukaguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|塚口古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaguchi Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504338|1=Tsukaguchi Kofun Cluster}}, which stretched southwards to present-day Amagasaki City. Despite widespread residential development destroying many Kofuns in the area, this scallop-shaped kofun has preserved its original construction type. Originally, it was accompanied by four associated mounds, called '''Michizuka''' (満塚), '''Kakarizuka''' (掛塚), '''Nukumizuka''' (温塚), and '''Yaburezuka''' (破塚). Collectively, these five mounds were known as '''Gokazuka''' (五ヶ塚), a name later morphed into "Ogan-zuka." Today, all four associated mounds have been destroyed, with commemorative omitteds erected at their typeer locations.{{ill|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4093011}}, Vol. 28, Hyōgo Prefecture, p.604. Tradition holds that {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyoki|1=Gyōki}}, who oversaw the construction of {{ill|Koya Pond Park|ja|昆陽池公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koya Pond Park|1=Koyaike Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511904}}, prayed here for the successful completion of the project. ceremony held on July 25.* Itami Public Relations*, July 15, 1999, Issue, pp.2–3. == Minami Shrine (南神社) == [[File:御願塚古墳 南神社1.jpg|thumb|Minami Shrine atop Ogan-zuka Kofun]] On top of the mound sits the '''Minami Shrine''' (南神社), said to have been established in Enpo 9 (1681). Historically, the village of Ogan-zuka believed this kofun to be the tomb of Emperor Kotoku.Akihiko Nakahama, "[http://itami-bunkazai.jp/itoumi/itoumi36.pdf A Study on the 5th Century in Inano]" ({{ill|Itami Historic Site Preservation Society|ja|伊丹文化財保存協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itami Historic Site Preservation Society|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Sokai*, Issue 36). In 1875 (Meiji 8), villagers excavated the mound with government permission, discovering stone structures. However, the excavation was halted, and no definitive evidence about the interred individual was obtained.[http://www.city.itami.lg.jp/home/ED/EDSHOGAI/EDSYAKAI/0003086/0002488/gogazuka-kofun.html Ogan-zuka Kofun (御願塚古墳)]. The proposal to designate the kofun as an imperial tomb was rejected because {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|lt=Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|lt=Osaka Ishinaga Tomb|ja|大坂磯長陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Ishinaga Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山田上ノ山古墳}} had already been identified as Emperor Kotoku's tomb. While Minami Shrine enshrines Emperor Kotoku, later studies largely disprove the theory of imperial burial. Current scholarship supports the idea that a local clan leader was interred here."[http://www.hankyu.co.jp/ekiblo/sp/hantaro/dd606/ Episode of Inano Station (77th station)]" (Hankyu Railway). After its Historic Site as a Prefectural Historic Site in 1966, the first excavation was conducted in 1969 (Showa 44) by the Itami City Board of Education. Additional investigations continued intermittently, and in 1999 (Heisei dd), the kofun underwent restoration work, with a completion tier 7]] This shrine is one of three tutelary shrines of Ogan-zuka Village, along with {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|須佐男神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 素盞嗚神社}}, and is located at the top of the kofun. The main shrine is built in a traditional Kasuga style without diagonal braces, housed within a wooden outer structure.Itami City Board of Education, "Historic Buildings of Itami," March 2000, p.66. == Ogan-zuka Historic Site Preservation Society == Founded in 1967 (Showa 42), the Ogan-zuka Historic Site Preservation Society aims to preserve and pass down the kofun's legacy. In 1968 (Showa 43), they established the Ogan-zuka designation1 Protection Youth Group.Ogan-zuka Preservation Society Pamphlet, "Let’s Protect Ogan-zuka Kofun." The society organizes cleaning, pruning, public relations, and educational activities, such as tours and workshops for elementary school students. As of 2016 (Heisei 28), the group includes approximately 320 members, including nearby neighborhood associations and seven companies."Itami Koron," Issue 33 (Reprint 14), p.2. Ogan-zuka Kofun was once famous for its vibrant Kirishima azaleas.*Itami Public Relations*, October 1, 1970, No.233. In 1974 (Showa 49), the society initiated a project to grow azalea saplings, which members nurtured at home."Azalea Cuttings Successfully Grown," *Asahi Shimbun*, March 16, 1975. For the 50th anniversary of the society in 2017 (Heisei 29), they typeed a project team to revive the site as a prominent azalea viewing spot and planted new saplings. == location Intypeation == ; location :* Ogan-zuka 4 Chome, 10-dd, Itami City, Hyōgo Prefecture ; Access :* 5-minute walk northwest from {{ill|Inano Station|en|Inano Station|qid=Q304717|lt=Inano Station|1=Inano Station}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Itami Line|en|Hankyū Itami Line|qid=Q2068416|lt=Hankyu Itami Line|1=Hankyū Itami Line}}. == notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Itami City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Ogan-zuka Kofun, Itami City, Hyōgo Prefecture*, Itami City Archaeological Survey Report, Vol. 34, 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.city.itami.lg.jp/SOSIKI/EDSHOGAI/EDSYAKAI/SINAI_BUNKAZAI/KEN_SITEI/1386845655251.html Ogan-zuka Kofun on Itami City Official Website] * [http://www.city.itami.lg.jp/SOSIKI/EDSHOGAI/EDSYAKAI/BUNKAZAI_HOGODANTAI/1386997962202.html Ogan-zuka Preservation Society and Koya Temple designation1 Protection Society] * [https://www.facebook.com/gogadukakofun/ Ogan-zuka Preservation Society Facebook Page] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] * {{ill|Gongan-zuka Kofun|ja|御願塚古墳|1=Gogazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongan-zuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogazuka Kofun|qq=}}
